briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the collapse of Synapse, a fintech middleware firm, highlighting risks in the banking-as-a-service model.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is driving increasingly unconventional credit card products with novel rewards structures and spending mechanics.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple has acquired UK-based open banking startup Credit Kudos, signalling the tech giant's growing ambitions in financial services and credit decisioning.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep dive into how open banking frameworks are reshaping competitive dynamics in the payments industry.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Are Canadians reaching their 'breaking point'? New data shows more people filing for insolvency](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/insolvency-filings-increasing-canada-9.7197230?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — New data reveals a rising tide of insolvency filings across Canada, raising concerns about household financial resilience amid ongoing cost-of-living pressures.

- **[Canadian VC sees lowest quarterly deal count in nearly a decade](https://betakit.com/canadian-vc-sees-lowest-quarterly-deal-count-in-nearly-a-decade/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian venture capital activity hit its lowest quarterly deal count in almost ten years, signalling a challenging funding environment for startups including those in fintech.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada formally labelled screen scraping as insecure and set an open banking implementation target, a foundational moment in Canada's ongoing consumer-directed finance journey.

- **[Feds announce $66 million for 44 businesses through AI Compute Access Fund](https://betakit.com/feds-announce-66-million-for-44-businesses-through-ai-compute-access-fund/)** — *BetaKit* — The federal government announced $66 million in AI compute funding for 44 Canadian businesses, a development with potential downstream benefits for AI-driven fintech innovation.

- **[Rave takes fight with Apple over App Store removal to Canada's Competition Tribunal](https://betakit.com/rave-takes-fight-with-apple-over-app-store-removal-to-canadas-competition-tribunal/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian app company Rave is escalating its dispute with Apple to the Competition Tribunal, a case that could have broader implications for app-based fintech and payments distribution in Canada.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Are Canadians reaching their 'breaking point'? New data shows more people filing for insolvency](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/insolvency-filings-increasing-canada-9.7197230?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Rising insolvency rates across Canada are directly relevant to Atlantic Fintech's financial inclusion mandate, as Atlantic Canadian households often face amplified economic vulnerability and could benefit most from accessible, innovative financial tools.

- **[Canadian VC sees lowest quarterly deal count in nearly a decade](https://betakit.com/canadian-vc-sees-lowest-quarterly-deal-count-in-nearly-a-decade/)** — *BetaKit* — A near-decade low in Canadian VC deal activity underscores the ecosystem-building challenges facing Atlantic Canadian fintech startups seeking growth capital in an already constrained regional funding landscape.

- **['It has been devastating,' U.S. spirits group says about Canadian alcohol boycotts](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/nova-scotia/american-alcohol-boycotts-bans-canada-trade-war-nslc-nova-scotia-9.7194289?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Nova Scotia's NSLC is featured in this trade-war story, a reminder of how geopolitical and economic headwinds are reshaping consumer spending patterns in Atlantic Canada with implications for payments and financial services demand in the region.
